rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVINDER SINGH SIDHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/27/21. (No earlier date available.) Original bank statements must be E-filed and the attorney or fiduciary's verified statement re retention of the originals must be filed, as set forth in LRSF 2.11 M.4. and 14.93 D. A courtesy copy of the E-filed statements and the attorney's or administrator's verified declaration re retention of the originals must be received by the assigned Examiner by 1/15/21. Examiners will not review any unfiled papers. [LRSF 14.6 D.] Courtesy copies must be file stamped hard copies. Addendum (12/23/20): Original statements were dropped off, unfiled. Counsel should provide the Examiner a SASE large enough to accommodate them so that they can be returned to Counsel for e-filing with a cover sheet.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RICHARD PATUREL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/21 for the CIU report to be completed. There is STILL no such thing as an "errata" to an accounting petition. The Judicial Council recognizes either a SUPPLEMENT to a petition or an AMENDMENT TO a petition (or a fully AMENDED petition). [CRC 7.3.] An amending pleading requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).]

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine how to proceed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLENE D. LOWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/19/21. Tangible personal property was appraised by the administrator, when it should have been appraised by the Probate Referee. Assets that a personal representative may appraise are cash or cash equivalents. [Prob. C. Sect. 8901.] The petition does not address what happened with the household furnishings the administrator at $5,000. They are not part of the proposed distribution and no receipts have been filed. The petition does not set forth the net amount of cash distributable to each heir. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.67 L.7.] Attachment 1 to the petition shows that 1119 Gilman Street, Oakland, is to be distributed equally among the three heirs. The real property that was appraised in the I&A and is described on Exhibit A to the petition is 1119 Gilman Avenue, San Francisco. The proposed distribution on Attachment 1 to the petition also shows just a 33 1/3% interest in the two properties in Lake County to each of Anthony Lowe Sr. and Cassandra Duhon. Letters were issued on 11/18/29, not 11/8/19. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/21. If an amendment will be filed (which appears appropriate), Counsel might also wish to correct her fee request.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOROTHY M. JEFFRIES LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirmirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Petitioner alleges that venue is proper in San Francisco, as it is the principal place of administration; however, Petitioner was provided notice of this hearing in St. Helena. 2) Petitioner requests confirmation that "each asset described in Exhibit E to the petition and any other asset held in the name" of Settlor be confirmed to be a trust asset. Only two assets were identified in the Notice of Hearing. 3) Petitioner avers that although 480 Taplin was acquired by Settlor after the execution of the Trust, it should still be confirmed as a Trust asset pursuant to Settlor's intent that all of her asset be held by her trust. Attorney has not been able to find authority to show whether or not property acquired after the execution of a trust can be confirmed as a trust asset. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.
